summary: All 25 taint flows are FALSE POSITIVES. The XSS flows (001-024) all involve ternary expressions where user input is used only in boolean/equality checks and the output is always a hardcoded literal string ('active-item', 'check-pass', 'hidden', or ''). The user-controlled value is never echoed. The SSRF flow (001) uses a hardcoded destination host with the user input appended only as a URL-encoded query parameter, and the handler requires manage_options capability plus nonce verification.

false_positives.reason: The echo statement outputs only the literal string 'check-pass' or ''. The get_site_option('cookiebot-cbid') value is used solely as a boolean condition, never echoed. Additionally, this is an admin-only page (manage_network_options capability required).

sanitize_text_field applied to $_GET['tab'], then used in strict equality check ($active_tab === 'general-settings'). Output is always literal 'active-item' or ''. User input is never reflected in output. Admin-only page (manage_options).
Same pattern as xss-002. Strict equality ($active_tab === 'additional-settings') outputs only 'active-item' or ''. User input never reflected.

Not SSRF. The destination host is hardcoded to 'https://api.ea.prod.usercentrics.cloud'. User input ($code from $_POST['code']) is sanitized with sanitize_text_field(), URL-encoded with urlencode(), and appended only as a query parameter value. The attacker cannot control the destination host/path. Additionally, the handler requires manage_options capability AND nonce verification (check_ajax_referer).
